author: @Nick_Tag
text:
wow just a top result !  
when i first saw the video title i assumed u might be playing around with photogrammetry haha, now you got me thinking ...! 
btw have you heard about the Carbon fibre filament for 3d printers? I met a guy that printed custom titanium bike parts, might have been with SLS though (also I spoke to digits2widgets who initially laughed at my suggesting of car bumpers (which they now do - using an ∞ Z-axis setup)

author: @williwonti
text: You did paint that rather well.  We don't see these too much around here, but they seem to be a better deal if you actually refill them.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Yes, I always have a can of butane to refill these things, the oldest one I have is 12+ years old, it was given to me lol. Thank you mate! :)

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey

author: @sidneytwining1958
text:

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey

author: @Li0rE
text:
You're not using a standalone CAD product, but your slicer software has functionality that allows you to do "computer assisted design." 

The process for doing what you did in this video in a CAD software would be basically identical.

replies.author: @Li0rE
replies.text: My point is not to criticize you, just to point out that the valuable skill you are demonstrating here is mashing up existing open source designs - which is a great way to get jump started and less intimidating than trying to start from scratch.

replies.author: @TheWingnut58
replies.text:
Not disputing your point....but....
Everyone with a 3d printer is familiar with a slicer, while there are many who aren't with CAD...

replies.author: @Li0rE
replies.text: That is a good point. I am expressing that personally, I would make the highlight here: "CAD is not as difficult and intimidating as you might have thought" as opposed to "You can skip CAD altogether"

replies.author: @mkeyx82
replies.text: I somewhat agree with this comment. This tutorial did in fact involve (basic) CAD modelling work.

replies.author: @mkeyx82
replies.text: Based on the title of the video, I was kind of expecting to see some manual editing of text files :D

author: @dgpreston5593
text:
Nicely done, Luke...

I see you flipped one sword down-point.   You must have read my mind across time as I was watching, LOL

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahah I must have! I just didn't like that the swords were pointing the same way lol. i think it looks much better! Thanks mate! :)

replies.author: @dgpreston5593
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey I agree, asymmetry adds character.  If you are at all into programming, you might like OpenSCAD for making models to print.
